generations.code:
import sys

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    n = int(input_data[0])
    a = list(map(int, input_data[1:n+1]))
    
    MAX_VAL = 501
    MAX_XOR = 512
    
    # reach[v] = bitmask of XOR values achievable by increasing subsequences ending exactly at value v
    reach = [0] * MAX_VAL
    # prefix[v] = bitmask of XOR values achievable by increasing subsequences ending at value <= v
    prefix = [0] * MAX_VAL
    
    for val in a:
        prev = prefix[val - 1] if val > 0 else 0
        # new xor values: start fresh with val, or extend
        new_xors = (1 << val)  # just [val] alone
        # extend: for each x in prev, x ^ val
        # shift trick: we need to XOR each set bit position with val
        # This isn't a simple shift... need to iterate or use different approach
        for x in range(MAX_XOR):
            if prev & (1 << x):
                new_xors |= (1 << (x ^ val))
        
        added = new_xors & ~reach[val]
        if added:
            reach[val] |= added
            for v in range(val, MAX_VAL):
                prefix[v] |= added
    
    result = prefix[MAX_VAL - 1] | 1  # include empty subsequence (XOR = 0)
    ans = [i for i in range(MAX_XOR) if result & (1 << i)]
    print(len(ans))
    print(*ans)

solve()

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    INF = float('inf')
    dp = [INF] * 512
    dp[0] = -1
    
    for val in a:
        for x in range(512):
            if dp[x] < val:
                new_x = x ^ val
                if dp[new_x] > val:
                    dp[new_x] = val
    
    result = [x for x in range(512) if dp[x] < INF]
    print(len(result))
    print(*result)

solve()

bugs.description: The solution runs in O(n * MAX_XOR) time (≈ 5·10⁷ iterations) plus additional O(MAX_VAL * MAX_XOR) updates for the prefix array. With n up to 1e� this is far too slow for Python and will TLE on the worst‑case input.

The solution runs in O(n * MAX_XOR) time (≈ 5·10⁷ iterations) plus additional O(MAX_VAL * MAX_XOR) updates for the prefix array. With n up to 1e� this is far too slow for Python and will TLE on the worst‑case input.

fixes.code:
import sys

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    n = int(input_data[0])
    a = list(map(int, input_data[1:n+1]))
    
    MAX_VAL = 501
    # dp[v] = bitmask of XOR values for increasing subsequences ending at value v
    dp = [0] * MAX_VAL
    
    # empty subsequence gives XOR = 0
    all_xor = 1  # bit 0 set
    
    for val in range(n):
        v = a[val]
        # compute prefix OR of dp[0..v-1]
        prefix_xor = 0
        for u in range(v):
            prefix_xor |= dp[u]
        # new xor values: extend existing subsequences
        new_mask = prefix_xor  # these are x values, XOR with v gives new
        # shift: for each bit x set in new_mask, set bit (x^v)
        # We need to apply XOR v to all set bits - use the xor_mask trick
        new_vals = 0
        for x in range(512):
            if new_mask & (1 << x):
                new_vals |= (1 << (x ^ v))
        new_vals |= (1 << v)  # single element
        dp[v] |= new_vals
        all_xor |= dp[v]
    
    result = [x for x in range(512) if all_xor & (1 << x)]
    print(len(result))
    print(*result)

solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    n = int(input_data[0])
    a = list(map(int, input_data[1:n+1]))
    
    MAX_VAL = 501
    # dp[v] = bitmask of XOR values for increasing subsequences ending at value v
    dp = [0] * MAX_VAL
    
    # empty subsequence gives XOR = 0
    all_xor = 1  # bit 0 set
    
    for val in range(n):
        v = a[val]
        # compute prefix OR of dp[0..v-1]
        prefix_xor = 0
        for u in range(v):
            prefix_xor |= dp[u]
        # new xor values: extend existing subsequences
        new_mask = prefix_xor  # these are x values, XOR with v gives new
        # shift: for each bit x set in new_mask, set bit (x^v)
        # We need to apply XOR v to all set bits - use the xor_mask trick
        new_vals = 0
        for x in range(512):
            if new_mask & (1 << x):
                new_vals |= (1 << (x ^ v))
        new_vals |= (1 << v)  # single element
        dp[v] |= new_vals
        all_xor |= dp[v]
    
    result = [x for x in range(512) if all_xor & (1 << x)]
    print(len(result))
    print(*result)

solve()

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    INF = float('inf')
    dp = [INF] * 512
    dp[0] = -1
    
    for val in a:
        for x in range(512):
            if dp[x] < val:
                new_x = x ^ val
                if dp[new_x] > val:
                    dp[new_x] = val
    
    result = [x for x in range(512) if dp[x] < INF]
    print(len(result))
    print(*result)

solve()
